question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose alias field is crafted to be byte-identical to an existing active entity's alias, causing findByAlias() to resolve to the wrong entity ID and misdirect subsequent transaction processing to a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoCreate transaction with an alias that is exactly 20 bytes (EVM_ADDRESS_LENGTH), causing EntityIdServiceImpl to route the lookup through findByEvmAddress() instead of findByAlias(), bypassing alias-based entity resolution and resolving to a different entity than intended?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction referencing an alias whose corresponding entity has deleted=NULL in the database, exploiting the SQL semantics of 'deleted <> true' (which evaluates NULL <> true as NULL/false) to cause findByAlias() to return empty and trigger a recoverable error path that silently skips entity resolution, leading to incorrect transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractUpdate transaction with an EVM address in the body that matches an existing non-contract entity's evm_address column, causing findByEvmAddress() to resolve the wrong entity ID and overwrite that entity's type or metadata via updateContractType()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted EVM address that is a long-zero address (first 12 bytes zero), causing DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress() to resolve it to a numeric entity ID without hitting findByEvmAddress() at all, thereby bypassing the 'deleted <> true' guard and resolving to a deleted entity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that collides with the derived evm_address of an ECDSA secp256k1 alias (computed by aliasToEvmAddress()), causing findByEvmAddress() to resolve to an account that the attacker does not control, and allowing the attacker's transaction to reference that account's entity ID in downstream processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction containing sidecar ContractBytecode records with arbitrary contractId values, causing SidecarContractMigration to call updateContractType() with attacker-controlled entity IDs, thereby reclassifying non-contract entities (e.g., ACCOUNT or TOKEN) as CONTRACT type in the entity table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar records containing a very large number of contractId entries (approaching IN_CLAUSE_LIMIT=32767), causing updateContractType() to execute a bulk UPDATE affecting thousands of entity rows in a single database transaction, leading to table-level lock contention and denial of service for the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar records referencing the entity ID of a system account (e.g., id < 1000), causing updateContractType() to change the type of a system account to CONTRACT, which would then cause findByIdAndDeletedIsFalse() lookups in the web3 module to return the wrong entity type and break EVM execution for system contracts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes a large set of entity IDs to be queued for EVM address lookup via findEvmAddressesByIds(), where the IN clause contains attacker-controlled IDs, causing the query to scan the entity table for arbitrary IDs and leak EVM address mappings for entities the attacker should not be able to enumerate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that generates synthetic contract logs referencing long-zero addresses for entities with no evm_address, causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to return no mapping for those IDs, and the SyntheticLogListener to emit contract logs with incorrect (long-zero) topic addresses that misrepresent the true EVM address of the sender/receiver?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that triggers findEvmAddressesByIds() with a list containing duplicate entity IDs, causing the query to return duplicate EvmAddressMapping entries that overwrite each other in the SyntheticLogListener cache, leading to incorrect EVM address resolution for contract log topics?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an autoRenewAccountId alias that matches a deleted entity's alias, exploiting the 'deleted <> true' condition (which returns empty for deleted entities) to cause EntityIdServiceImpl.lookup() to return Optional.empty() and trigger Utility.handleRecoverableError(), silently skipping the autoRenewAccountId assignment and leaving the entity with a stale or null autoRenewAccountId?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoCreate transaction with an alias that is a valid ECDSA secp256k1 public key (35 bytes), causing the importer to derive an evm_address via aliasToEvmAddress() and populate entity.evm_address, then submit a second transaction referencing that derived EVM address via findByEvmAddress() to resolve to the newly created account without knowing its numeric ID?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCreate transaction whose receipt EVM address matches an existing account's evm_address in the entity table, causing findByEvmAddress() to resolve to the existing account's ID instead of the new contract's ID, and causing the importer to overwrite the existing account's entity record with CONTRACT type metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias byte array of length zero or one, causing findByAlias() to execute a query with an empty/minimal byte array that matches no entity but still incurs a full index scan on the alias column, enabling a low-cost repeated query to degrade importer performance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar records containing a contractId that maps to a TOKEN entity ID, causing updateContractType() to change the token entity's type to CONTRACT, which would then cause token-related queries (e.g., balance lookups, transfer processing) to fail or return incorrect results because the entity type no longer matches TOKEN?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that is identical to another entity's evm_address bytes (not alias), causing a mismatch between findByAlias() (which queries the alias column) and findByEvmAddress() (which queries the evm_address column), leading to inconsistent entity resolution depending on which lookup path is taken by EntityIdServiceImpl?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that is exactly 20 bytes but all zeros (0x0000...0000), causing findByEvmAddress() to return empty (no entity has a zero EVM address), but DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress() to also return null, leading to a recoverable error being logged and the transaction's contract reference being silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that is a valid protobuf-encoded key (e.g., Ed25519 key bytes), causing findByAlias() to match an existing account that was created with that key as its alias, and then use the resolved entity ID to associate the attacker's transaction with the victim account in entity_transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar records that include a contractId with shard/realm/num values that encode to an entity ID of 0 or a negative long, causing updateContractType() to execute 'UPDATE entity SET type=CONTRACT WHERE id IN (0)' and potentially corrupt the entity with ID 0 (if it exists) or cause unexpected SQL behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that is a very large byte array (e.g., thousands of bytes), causing findByAlias() to execute a parameterized query with an oversized byte parameter that causes excessive memory allocation in the database driver or triggers a query plan regression on the alias index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that is not exactly 20 bytes (e.g., 19 or 21 bytes), causing findByEvmAddress() to execute a query that never matches (since evm_address is stored as exactly 20 bytes), silently returning empty and causing the transaction handler to skip entity resolution without any error, leading to incomplete transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an alias that matches an entity whose deleted field is NULL (not false), exploiting the difference between 'deleted <> true' (used in importer's EntityRepository) and 'deleted is not true' (used in graphql's EntityRepository), causing the same alias to resolve differently depending on which module processes the lookup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an EVM address that matches an entity whose deleted field is NULL, exploiting the 'deleted <> true' condition (NULL <> true = NULL = false in SQL WHERE) to cause findByEvmAddress() to return empty for a logically active entity, leading to a recoverable error and incorrect transaction processing?
